criteria.description: claims.py exists at the voice-tutor repo root and exposes a pure decomposition function that takes document text (str) as input and returns a list of claim records. Each record is a structured object/dict exposing at minimum three fields: a stable id, the claim text, and a source anchor (the supporting quote/passage from the document). 'Ordered' has concrete semantics: the returned records preserve the order of the claims as they appear in the model's response — the parser must NOT reorder, sort, or dedupe them. The parser reads the model's text payload from an Anthropic SDK-shaped response object (resp.content[0].text, mirroring documents.py), not a pre-parsed dict fed directly to the parser.
criteria.verifyBy: In tests/test_claims.py, import the decomposition function from claims and call it on a fixture document with the Anthropic client patched at the messages.create boundary to return an SDK-shaped object exposing .content[0].text carrying a known multi-claim payload whose claim texts are in a deliberately NON-sorted, distinguishable order. Assert (a) the return is a list whose elements each expose id, claim text, and source anchor fields (non-empty text, id present), and (b) the ordered sequence of claim texts in the returned records is exactly equal to that non-sorted mocked sequence (and NOT equal to its sorted or reversed variant), so 'no reordering' is falsifiable.

criteria.description: The decomposition core constructs/uses the Anthropic client lazily inside the function call (mirroring documents.py _generate_summary), never at module scope, and reads no API key at module scope. Importing claims performs no network I/O and requires no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
criteria.verifyBy: A test imports claims with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset and asserts the import succeeds. A separate test parses claims.py with the ast module (mirroring conftest.py's existing ast usage) and asserts there is no module-scope (top-level) call constructing the Anthropic client (e.g. anthropic.Anthropic(...) / Anthropic(...)) and no module-scope read of an API key from os.environ — such calls/reads occur only inside a function body.

criteria.description: claims.py import closure is minimal: it imports ONLY the anthropic SDK and the Python standard library. It must NOT import bot.py, app.py, pipecat, fastapi, or other heavy runtime dependencies.
criteria.verifyBy: Static check in tests/test_claims.py reads claims.py source (via ast import inspection or source scan) and asserts no import of bot, app, pipecat, or fastapi; and that `import claims` succeeds under the verifier env (anthropic + stdlib only).

criteria.description: Claim ids follow a frozen, purely positional scheme: the id of the Nth claim (0-based index i) in the returned list is deterministically derived from its position (f"c{i+1}" -> c1, c2, c3, ...), independent of claim text content. Ids are unique within a returned claim set.
criteria.verifyBy: Test asserts, for a mocked response of >=3 claims, that the returned ids equal the frozen positional literal [c1, c2, c3, ...] for that length; and asserts len(set(ids)) == len(ids) and all ids non-empty for each fixture.

criteria.description: Positive anchor property, driven by the REAL committed fixtures: for at least one committed fixture document under tests/fixtures/claims/, the decomposition function is invoked end-to-end with the Anthropic client mocked, and EVERY returned claim's source anchor is a verbatim substring of THAT fixture file's actual text as read from tests/fixtures/claims/ (not of a synthetic/inline string). The mocked payload's anchors are taken from the fixture text so the fixtures are genuinely exercised through the decomposition assertions.
criteria.verifyBy: Test reads a fixture .txt from tests/fixtures/claims/ into a variable, builds the mocked model response whose anchors are verbatim substrings drawn from that fixture text, calls the decomposition function, and asserts every returned claim's anchor satisfies `anchor in fixture_text` where fixture_text is the string read from the committed fixture file. Repeat/parametrize across the three fixtures for at least the substring property.

criteria.description: The Anthropic prompt/instructions in claims.py encode the required granularity, referencing BOTH (a) the 1-3 spoken-sentence articulable-claim framing (not one-per-sentence, not chapter-level themes) AND (b) the approximate ~10-40 claim count target. This is intentionally a static prompt-content check because the LLM is mocked in the hermetic suite and granularity cannot be asserted behaviorally under a mock.
criteria.verifyBy: Static read of claims.py asserts, via tolerant concept-level matching (NOT an exact fixed phrase): a regex matching the 1-3 sentence idea (e.g. allowing '1-3'/'1 to 3'/'one to three' followed by 'sentence'), AND a mention of the count range (e.g. both the numbers 10 and 40 appearing in the prompt's count guidance). Both concepts must be present.

criteria.description: The parser validates the model's structured response and RAISES (chosen and frozen failure mode) on malformed output — specifically a missing/empty claim text, a missing/empty source anchor, OR a source anchor that is not a verbatim substring of the input document text — rather than silently skipping or emitting invalid records. This criterion owns all raise-conditions.
criteria.verifyBy: Test feeds three malformed mocked responses — (i) missing/empty claim text, (ii) missing/empty anchor, (iii) anchor not present in the document — and asserts the function raises an exception for each; and asserts a well-formed response parses without raising into validated records.

criteria.description: The three real source documents are copied into committed in-repo fixtures under tests/fixtures/claims/ (using the three provided doc ids as filenames), the fixtures are non-empty extracted-text files (not zero-byte placeholders), and the tests read those committed copies — never the ~/.voice-tutor/documents path.
criteria.verifyBy: tests/fixtures/claims/ contains the three fixture .txt files named by the three provided doc ids, each non-empty (size > 0 and containing document prose); a read of tests/test_claims.py shows it reads from tests/fixtures/claims/ and contains no reference to ~/.voice-tutor or an absolute documents path.

criteria.description: tests/test_claims.py is the single hermetic test file for this work and passes with no network and no API key: it patches/mocks the Anthropic client so that no real messages.create is invoked, no test reads or sets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and mocked-decomposition assertions are driven by the committed fixture documents.
criteria.verifyBy: Read tests/test_claims.py and assert: it is the only added hermetic test file; the Anthropic client symbol used by claims.py (e.g. anthropic.Anthropic) is patched via unittest.mock so the real network client is never constructed and messages.create is never reached over the network; no test reads or sets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from/in os.environ; and decomposition assertions consume tests/fixtures/claims/ documents. pytest is NOT added to pyproject.toml.

scope.description: Work stays in claims.py (repo root), tests/test_claims.py, and tests/fixtures/claims/. Do not modify bot.py, app.py, documents.py, or any Pipecat/FastAPI/UI wiring.
No scoring of transcripts against claims, no conversation steering prompts, and no live-session/Pipecat integration — explicitly out of scope for this sprint.

This sprint delivers ONLY the pure decomposition core; the .claims.json sidecar write helper is deferred to a later persistence sprint. Do not ship partial/half-built sidecar or DOCUMENTS_DIR write logic in claims.py for this sprint. Do not add pytest or other test deps to pyproject.toml (verifier supplies pytest via `uv run --with pytest`).

criteria.description: claims.py adds a write helper that serializes a claim set to a sidecar next to the document at (claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR resolved at call time) / f"{doc_id}.claims.json", mirroring documents.py's sidecar PLACEMENT/NAMING pattern (sidecar beside the doc, path computed via a small path-helper, generate-once) — NOT necessarily its text format. The output is human-readable: indented, multi-line JSON (not a single-line compact blob). The exact JSON envelope (bare top-level list vs an object wrapping the claims) is an implementation choice and is NOT constrained; only human-readability and round-trip fidelity are required.
criteria.verifyBy: In tests/test_claims.py, redirect claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR into tmp_path, call the write helper with a fixture-derived claim set, and assert (a) the file exists at tmp_path / f"{doc_id}.claims.json"; (b) json.loads(sidecar.read_text()) succeeds; (c) the raw text is multi-line and indented (contains "\n" and leading-space indentation), so a compact single-line json.dumps output would fail.

criteria.description: The persisted sidecar round-trips field-for-field into the same claim records: reading the sidecar back and extracting the claim dicts yields exactly [c.to_dict() for c in claims] (each {id, claim, anchor}), regardless of the JSON envelope chosen in c1. Equality is defined against the dict serialization, not dataclass identity.
criteria.verifyBy: In tests/test_claims.py, write a fixture-derived claim set, json.load the sidecar, extract the list of claim dicts (unwrapping the envelope if the module uses one), and assert it equals [c.to_dict() for c in claims]. The assertion is written envelope-agnostically so it holds whether the top level is a bare list or an object with a claims key.

criteria.description: Generate-once-per-doc semantics via a get-or-create entry point keyed by doc_id: it takes at least (doc_id, document_text), computes the sidecar path from claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR at call time, and — when the sidecar file already exists ON DISK — reconstructs and returns the claim records from that file WITHOUT invoking the Anthropic client; when the sidecar is absent it performs the (mocked) LLM decomposition and writes the sidecar. The return TYPE is identical on both the miss path and the cache-hit path: both return list[Claim] (the hit path reconstructs Claim records from the sidecar, not raw dicts).
criteria.verifyBy: In tests/test_claims.py, with the Anthropic client patched to a call-counting mock and claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR redirected to tmp_path: (a) a first call for an uncached doc invokes the Anthropic mock exactly once, creates the sidecar file on disk, and returns a list of Claim instances; (b) prove the hit path uses on-disk state (not in-process memoization) by clearing/bypassing any in-module cache or invoking a freshly-evaluated call, then a second call for the same doc_id increments the Anthropic mock call count by zero; (c) the second call returns list[Claim] whose [c.to_dict() for c in result] equals that of the first call (same ids, claim text, anchors) and every element isinstance(_, Claim) — so a cache-hit returning stale/empty claims or dicts fails.

criteria.description: The miss-path get-or-create result is consistent with what it persisted: after the first (miss) generate call, the sidecar on disk, read back and unwrapped, equals [c.to_dict() for c in returned] — linking the persistence layer to the returned records (Definition-of-Done linkage).
criteria.verifyBy: In tests/test_claims.py, after the first (miss) generate call from c3, json.load the sidecar, unwrap to the claim-dict list, and assert it equals [c.to_dict() for c in first_call_result].

criteria.description: claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR is defined in claims.py itself (mirroring documents.py's Path.home()/".voice-tutor"/"documents", resolved at call time) and is redirectable; it must NOT be imported/re-exported from documents.py. Redirecting claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR causes ALL sidecar writes to land under the redirected path and never in the real ~/.voice-tutor/documents directory.
criteria.verifyBy: In tests/test_claims.py, monkeypatch claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR to a tmp_path (via conftest fixture extension or in-test monkeypatch — NOT relying on the existing documents.DOCUMENTS_DIR fixture), run the write/generate tests, assert written files appear only under tmp_path, and use a before/after snapshot of the real ~/.voice-tutor/documents directory to assert it is byte-for-byte unchanged.

criteria.description: The hermetic suite passes with no network and no API key: the Anthropic call is mocked, no API key is read at import time, and claims.py's import closure is limited to the Anthropic SDK plus the standard library. In particular claims.py must NOT import documents.py, pypdf, bot.py, app.py, pipecat, or fastapi (importing documents.py would drag pypdf into the closure).
criteria.verifyBy: Run `uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_claims.py -q` in a fresh worktree with no API key and no network and confirm all tests pass; additionally, an in-suite AST/import inspection of claims.py asserts its top-level import names are a subset of {anthropic} ∪ stdlib modules, explicitly forbidding documents, pypdf, bot, app, pipecat, and fastapi.

scope.description: Production changes stay within claims.py at the voice-tutor repo root; hermetic tests stay within tests/test_claims.py, using committed fixtures under tests/fixtures/claims/ and the conftest.py DOCUMENTS_DIR-redirection mechanism (extended to add a claims.DOCUMENTS_DIR redirect, since the existing fixture only governs documents.DOCUMENTS_DIR).
No wiring into bot.py, app.py, Pipecat, FastAPI, or any live-session/UI code; do not add pytest to pyproject.toml; do not import documents.py or pypdf from claims.py.

Out of scope: scoring transcripts against claims, steering prompts, and decomposition/granularity prompt tuning beyond what caching/persistence requires. The pure decomposition function already exists from prior work; this sprint only adds the persistence + generate-once layer around it.
